purpose: Multi-line free-form text entry: a light-DOM element wrapping a real <textarea> inside its own label. No affixes, no masking.
useWhen: Input is prose-length or naturally multi-line: notes, descriptions, messages, feedback.

notWhen.case: Single-line values: names, emails, search terms.
The value has a fixed printed format.

guidance: A placeholder is never a substitute for label content — the accessible name comes from the default children or an aria-label.
`rows` sets the initial visible height; size the box for the length of answer you expect.

default.description: The visible label content (the accessible name via the wrapping label).

keyboard.does: Inserts a newline — never submits the form (native textarea behavior, preserved).

examples.title: Length-constrained
examples.why: minlength/maxlength forward to native constraint validation.

examples.title: Placeholder as label
examples.html: <zbk-textarea name="feedback" placeholder="Tell us what you think"></zbk-textarea>
examples.why: No accessible name: placeholders vanish on entry and are not announced as the name. Give it label children or aria-label.
